_comment: FALSE POSITIVE — SP-750001: Legitimate newsletter where sender display name contains 'CEO'
scenario: Legitimate newsletter from a business publication whose editor-in-chief title appears in display name

event.Subject: CEO Today: Leadership Insights for Q1 2024

why_fp: Sender domain hbr.org has SPF+DKIM+DMARC pass — authenticated sender
Display name 'HBR CEO Today Newsletter' — 'CEO' is part of publication name, not impersonation
Recipient is the actual CEO, not finance — not a wire fraud target
Known newsletter subscription (check user's inbox rules for recurring sender)
tuning_recommendation: Allowlist authenticated senders (DMARC pass) from known business publications
Tighten the display-name regex to: '^(CEO|CFO|COO|CTO|Chief Executive|President)\s+[A-Z][a-z]+'
Require both: display-name impersonation AND external sender domain mismatch
